items: A progress briefing for Steven Lowe, Vice President, Finance
Engagement commenced {{fact:f:E-2009-002.start}}
Prepared by the engagement team at Key Group's request
Brenda Rodriguez, Senior Consultant, and Daniel Leach, Research Associate
Where Key Group's growth comes from over the next several years, and what it takes to capture it
Read the current position: where the company earns today, by segment and by customer
Size the market the company serves and the near segments it does not
Frame the growth options and rank them on return and on what the company can execute
We start from your own segment numbers, not from a template
Daniel Leach is sizing the market from published sources and your figures, in parallel with the current-state work
Interviews with the four segment leads run through this phase
Every figure we show ties back to one your finance team already recognizes

blocks.text: What We Are Hearing So Far

items: Growth in the two largest segments looks concentrated in a handful of accounts, not spread across the base
The nearest adjacent segments are larger than the core, but more contested
Geographic extension comes up repeatedly in the interviews, without a clear read yet on what it would cost
These are early observations from three of four interviews, offered as observations and not yet as findings
Current-position picture built to the segment level; customer detail in hand for the largest segment
Market sizing under way, roughly half complete
Three of the four segment-lead interviews done
No growth options are on the table yet, and none should be read into this briefing
Close the last interview and finish the market sizing
Frame three or four growth options, with their requirements and returns, for discussion and not for decision
Bring the current-position read and the option set to the next working session
Questions to Brenda Rodriguez; data questions direct to Daniel Leach
